title: BLOCKING: confirm derivative permission and exact attribution for each seed source
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual builds an open, multilingual, plain-language glossary of oncology and clinical-trial terms for patients and caregivers. Before any source definitions can be drafted (draft-001), every seed source must have confirmed and recorded permission for derivative plain-language definitions and the exact required attribution. This is a hard blocking gate: draft-001 must not begin until license-000 passes.
objective: For each seed source used by draft-001, confirm and record in writing on its allow-list entry: (a) the source's terms permit a derivative plain-language definition, and (b) the exact required attribution string. Exclude any source that cannot be confirmed.
acceptanceCriteria: For each seed source used by draft-001, the term-source-allow-list.yaml entry records in writing: (a) confirmation that the license permits a derivative plain-language definition, and (b) the exact required attribution string
Any seed source that cannot be confirmed as permitting derivatives is marked excluded with an exclusionReason, and draft-001 uses only confirmed sources
draft-001 does not start until this task is complete and the allow-list entries are updated
Confirmation is recorded with verifiedBy and verifiedDate on each entry
resources: NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms — confirm current reuse/derivative terms
ClinicalTrials.gov Learn About Studies glossary — confirm current reuse/derivative terms
MedlinePlus content-use notices — per-item verification required
output: Updated sources/term-source-allow-list.yaml with confirmed derivative-permission and exact attribution recorded on each seed source entry, plus a brief written confirmation note attached to the PR
